intro: Stega (Lavatera triloba) is a flowering plant species of the mallow family (Malvaceae), native to the Mediterranean region and often cultivated as an ornamental due to its attractive purple flowers and delicate foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Stega include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Stega is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make salves and infusions for topical application and to support overall health and well-being.
